shared_properties: Every tile snaps to a 16x16 px grid and keeps collision silhouettes easy to read during high-speed descent.
High-friction stone, low-friction slick floor, mid-friction moss floor, and safe ledge surfaces all stay within one dungeon palette family.
differences: This tileset packs four gameplay surface classes with explicit material contrast so players can read friction state without UI text.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Exact wall, floor, and ledge friction roles plus 16x16 tile size grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Stone wear, damp moss, and cold slick-floor contrast grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down S3 GDD
reference_images.purpose: Surface material list and safety ledge function grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use bevy_ecs_tilemap with one texture sheet and per-tile metadata that tags friction coefficients in gameplay code.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/friction_surface_tileset.png into the tilemap texture atlas and bind material metadata from a sidecar table keyed by tile index.
runtime_notes: Reserve tile groups for stone wall, slick floor, moss floor, and one-way ledge states.
Do not introduce sub-pixel edges because the project uses nearest-neighbor scaling.

context.role: Core collision art for walls, floors, and ledges that define friction-controlled descent routes.
context.usage: Used in every stage segment to communicate surface friction before player contact.

deliverables.notes: Sixteen columns by seven rows of 16x16 tiles covering wall, floor, ledge, and transition variants.
Tile index map that labels stone_wall, slick_floor, moss_floor, and safe_ledge groups.

acceptance_criteria: Tileset size is exactly 256x112 px and every tile aligns to a 16x16 px grid.
Stone wall, slick floor, moss floor, and safe ledge groups are visually distinct under grayscale preview and color preview.
Legend file names the four surface classes with indices that match the authored sheet layout.
